summary: In this essay, Eric S. Lander traces the 20-year history of CRISPR's discovery and development. He uses this narrative to highlight the complex, collaborative, and often serendipitous ecosystem of scientific progress, arguing against the myth of the lone genius and emphasizing the value of understanding the circuitous path of discovery.

principles.statement: The human stories behind scientific advances are essential for understanding how progress actually happens.
principles.rationale: Understanding the circuitous path of discovery provides a realistic picture of scientific careers for trainees, informs funding agencies about how to invest, and dispels public misconceptions about how science works.
principles.source_quote: Yet, the human stories behind scientific advances can teach us a lot about the miraculous ecosystem that drives biomedical progress—about the roles of serendipity and planning, of pure curiosity and practical application, of hypothesis-free and hypothesis-driven science, of individuals and teams, and of fresh perspectives and deep expertise.

principles.statement: Scientific progress relies on a diverse and interconnected ecosystem of approaches.
principles.rationale: Breakthroughs do not come from a single method, but from a combination of basic curiosity, practical industrial application, bioinformatics, and biochemical experimentation across different fields and geographies.
principles.source_quote: Most of all, the Perspective describes an inspiring ensemble of a dozen or so scientists who—with their collaborators and other contributors whose stories are not elaborated here—discovered the CRISPR system, unraveled its molecular mechanisms, and repurposed it...

mental_models.name: The Ecosystem of Scientific Discovery
mental_models.description: Viewing scientific progress not as a linear march of isolated, predictable breakthroughs, but as a complex, interconnected ecosystem that requires a balance of serendipity, planning, basic curiosity, applied science, and diverse teams.
mental_models.example: The 20-year journey of CRISPR, which required obscure archaea research in Spain, yogurt fermentation troubleshooting in France, and computational biology at the NIH, all converging to create a revolutionary genome-editing tool.

signature_quotes.text: Yet, the human stories behind scientific advances can teach us a lot about the miraculous ecosystem that drives biomedical progress—about the roles of serendipity and planning, of pure curiosity and practical application, of hypothesis-free and hypothesis-driven science, of individuals and teams, and of fresh perspectives and deep expertise.
signature_quotes.context: Explaining why the history of the CRISPR discovery is important to document.

signature_quotes.text: Once a fact is firmly established, the circuitous path that led to its discovery is seen as a distraction.
signature_quotes.context: Observing how scientists typically view the history of their own field.

signature_quotes.text: It is also important for a general public who often imagines scientists as lone geniuses cloistered in laboratories.
signature_quotes.context: Arguing for the importance of sharing the collaborative reality of scientific discovery.

anti_patterns.description: Ignoring the history of science and the circuitous path of discovery once a fact is firmly established.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It deprives trainees of realistic career guides and misleads the public and funding agencies about the unpredictable, serendipitous nature of how scientific breakthroughs actually occur.
It fails to recognize that scientific breakthroughs are the result of an 'inspiring ensemble' of scientists, collaborators, and diverse contributors building on each other's work across the globe.
